items.question: Do you work on all makes and models?
items.answer: Yes. All Model Repair services domestic, European, Asian, and luxury vehicles — including BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, Porsche, Lexus, Toyota, Honda, Nissan, Ford, Chevrolet, and more.

items.question: Where is All Model Repair located?
items.answer: We're in Glendale, California, at 1305 S Glendale Ave #1 — serving Glendale, Atwater Village, Burbank, Eagle Rock, Pasadena, Silver Lake, and greater Los Angeles.

items.question: Do I need an appointment for auto repair?
items.answer: Appointments are recommended, but walk-ins are welcome depending on shop availability. Calling ahead helps us diagnose issues faster and reduce wait times.

items.question: How long does a diagnostic take?
items.answer: Most diagnostics begin the same day. Timing depends on the issue, vehicle complexity, and parts availability.

items.question: Do you provide estimates before work begins?
items.answer: Yes. We provide clear estimates before repairs are performed, so you understand the scope of work and expected costs before we touch a wrench.

items.question: Need a tow?
items.answer: Can't get to us? We work hand-in-hand with a trusted local towing partner, Hovik Towing, who can bring your vehicle straight to our shop — then we take it from there. Call Hovik Towing at (818) 268-9998.

items.question: Why is my check engine light on?
items.answer: A check engine light can indicate anything from a loose gas cap to engine, ignition, emissions, or sensor-related issues. Diagnostic scanning helps identify the exact cause.

items.question: How often should I change my oil in LA traffic?
items.answer: Most vehicles driving regularly in Glendale and Los Angeles traffic should get oil changes every 5,000–7,500 miles, depending on oil type, driving habits, heat exposure, and manufacturer recommendations.

items.question: Why are my brakes squeaking?
items.answer: Brake squeaking may result from worn brake pads, dust buildup, rotor wear, moisture, or hardware issues. A brake inspection can determine whether replacement is needed.

items.question: What causes a car to overheat in Southern California?
items.answer: Common causes include coolant leaks, radiator problems, failing thermostats, water pump issues, or extreme heat combined with stop-and-go traffic conditions.

items.question: Do you service European cars in Glendale?
items.answer: Yes. We service European vehicles including BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, Volkswagen, Porsche, MINI, and Land Rover.

items.question: Do you do smog checks?
items.answer: We handle the diagnosis and repairs that get your vehicle ready to pass California's smog inspection — including check-engine, emissions, and catalytic converter issues. If your car needs a smog repair before certification, that's exactly what we do. Call us at (818) 548-8242 and we'll let you know the fastest way to get you smog-ready.

items.question: My car failed smog — can you help?
items.answer: Absolutely. Failed smog is one of our specialties, including catalytic converter repair. We'll find the real cause, give you a fair price, and get you passing.

items.question: What is ceramic coating?
items.answer: Ceramic coating is a protective liquid polymer applied to a vehicle's exterior that helps protect paint from UV exposure, contaminants, oxidation, and minor surface damage while enhancing gloss.

items.question: How long does ceramic coating last?
items.answer: Depending on the product and maintenance, ceramic coatings can last several years.

items.question: What's included in a full car detailing service?
items.answer: Full detailing may include exterior washing, paint decontamination, waxing or ceramic protection, wheel cleaning, interior vacuuming, leather treatment, stain removal, and trim restoration.

items.question: Is detailing worth it in Los Angeles?
items.answer: Yes. Sun exposure, dust, freeway driving, tree sap, and pollution in Los Angeles can wear down paint and interiors quickly. Regular detailing helps preserve appearance and vehicle value.

items.question: Can detailing remove scratches?
items.answer: Light scratches and swirl marks can often be improved or removed through paint correction. Deep scratches may require touch-up or repainting.

items.question: Do you use OEM or aftermarket parts?
items.answer: We can source OEM or high-quality aftermarket parts depending on your vehicle's needs and your preferences.

items.question: Can I bring my car in for a second opinion?
items.answer: Yes. Many customers visit us for second opinions on repair recommendations or pricing.

items.question: Do you help maintain older vehicles?
items.answer: Yes. We service both newer and older vehicles, and can help owners maintain high-mileage cars safely and reliably.

items.question: What makes you different from a dealership?
items.answer: As an independent shop, we offer more direct communication, personalized service, faster turnaround, and more flexible repair options than a dealership — backed by 20+ years on Glendale Ave.

items.question: Will I get a price before work starts?
items.answer: Always. We diagnose the problem, explain it in plain language, and you approve the cost before we begin. No surprises — that's how we've kept customers for over 20 years.
